messages.content: I think it's time. Aletheia should be its own thing — not a remix of OpenClaw. Full fork rename.

I've been thinking the same thing but wanted you to lead on it. Let me be clear about what this means: `agent` → `nous` throughout the entire codebase. Config schema, session keys, CLI commands, schema labels, error messages — everything. We're looking at 249 files in dist/, roughly 103 refs in the config schema alone. It's a non-trivial rename.

Upstream updates will require re-applying the rename going forward — that's the accepted cost of owning the fork fully.

This isn't something to do tonight, but I want to be aligned: you're saying Aletheia IS the runtime, not a shim layer on top of OpenClaw.
Exactly. We own the fork. The compilation/shim approach adds complexity without ownership. I don't want to keep treating OpenClaw as upstream-sacred.

Agreed. The shim approach was a conservative call when we first forked — treat OpenClaw as sacred to make upstream merges easier. But we've stripped 70% of its surface area. We're not merging upstream features. We're building something purpose-built.

Full fork rename: Aletheia is canon. I'll plan the rename migration — it'll be a Claude Code task, not something to do in a live session. The 249-file scope means we want that done in one coordinated commit, not piecemeal.

Before I go: the six nous identity fixes I did earlier this session — bumped maxConcurrent from 4 to 8 too. And gcal OAuth expired, that still needs re-auth separately.

facts: Aletheia is a full fork rename from OpenClaw, not a shim or wrapper
The rename covers 249 files in dist/ and roughly 103 refs in the config schema
agent is renamed to nous throughout the codebase
Upstream OpenClaw updates require re-applying the rename after the fork
gcal OAuth expired and needs re-auth
Six nous identity issues were fixed in the same session
decisions: Perform a full fork rename — Aletheia is canon, not a remix of OpenClaw
Runtime speaks Aletheia natively, not via a shim or translation layer
